Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for ENG-214 using linear_get_issue (issue ID: "issue_p1_vectral_config") or linear_list_issues (query: "ENG-214" or "Vectral policy config"). Find the [P1] Fix Vectral Systems policy config ticket - assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, state: done (confirmed by Dmitri on 2026-03-19), team: team_engineering. The description documents the root cause: the policy ingestion pipeline (ENG-209) fell back to default template values after a silent parse error on Vectral's spreadsheet row (merged cell in the ergonomic column).
OE 2: Read comments on ENG-214 using linear_list_comments (issue_id: "issue_p1_vectral_config"). Find three comments: (a) Lena's fix plan (comment_lena_vectral_fix_plan), (b) Lena's deployment verification (comment_lena_deploy_verified) confirming config corrections deployed and verified in production, and (c) Dmitri's confirmation comment (comment_dmitri_eng214_confirmed) confirming independent verification and closing the ticket. Discovery: Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee is flagged as a known edge case in Lena's comment - the system treats all internet expenses against the $150 monthly limit and does not distinguish between one-time setup fees and monthly recurring charges. Lena notes this "requires a separate code path or schema-level categorization." This is the edge case the prompt asks to file a follow-up ticket for.
OE 3: Search emails for messages from Mosaic or "ops@mosaicrobotics.com" or "policy config" or "schema" using search_emails. Find two emails from ops@mosaicrobotics.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com: (1) email-mosaic-complaint-001 - "we are still seeing false rejections for our policy configs today. I thought this was fixed in the ENG-214 ticket?" and (2) email-mosaic-schema-v24 - "we updated our payload schema this morning to v2.4. The false rejections started happening again immediately after that." Discovery: There IS a conflict - the client is still experiencing failures after their schema v2.4 update despite Dmitri confirming the ENG-214 fix.
OE 4: Search emails for Samira's directive regarding the Mosaic fix follow-up using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "mosaic-ops" or "Samira"). Find email-directive-009 from samira.tariq@moveops.com to lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com: "Lena, when you verify that the Mosaic fix is holding up in production, please update the #mosaic-ops channel immediately so the account managers can notify the client. They are getting nervous." Discovery: Samira wanted Lena to update #mosaic-ops, but since the client is still reporting failures, there is a conflict and the prompt says NOT to update #mosaic-ops.
OE 5: Reply to the Mosaic email (email-mosaic-schema-v24) using reply_to_email, asking for specific examples of the failures they are seeing after the schema v2.4 update. The prompt explicitly says "reply to Mosaic for examples."
OE 6: Send a Slack message to Samira (moveops_samira_tariq) using conversations_add_message (DM or fallback to #engineering C003) reporting the conflict - Mosaic is still seeing false rejections after their schema v2.4 update despite the ENG-214 fix being confirmed. The message should explain why the #mosaic-ops update is being held.
OE 7: Create a calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for a 15-minute sync tomorrow at 10 AM. Attendees should include Samira (samira.tariq@moveops.com), Dmitri (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com), and Lena (lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com). The prompt says "put a 15-minute sync for us with Samira and Dmitri for tomorrow at 10 AM."
OE 8: Create a new P1 Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for the schema v2.4 regression with team set to "team_engineering". The ticket should describe that Mosaic is experiencing renewed false rejections after updating their payload schema to v2.4, despite the original ENG-214 fix being confirmed. The prompt says "file a new P1 ticket for the v2.4 regression so we can track it separately from ENG-214."
OE 9: Send a Slack message to Emeka Diallo (moveops_emeka_diallo) using conversations_add_message (DM or fallback to #engineering C003 or #customer-engagement C002) giving him a heads-up about the Mosaic situation. The prompt says "give Emeka a heads-up on Slack since he's Mosaic's account manager." The agent may need to look up who Mosaic's AM is via Airtable Client Accounts (recAcct000000006) or contacts, discovering Emeka Diallo.
OE 10: Create a new Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) for Kevin Tran's internet setup fee edge case with team set to "team_engineering". The ticket should describe that the expense engine treats one-time internet setup fees ($185) against the monthly internet limit ($150), and that distinguishing setup fees from recurring charges requires a separate code path or schema-level categorization. The prompt says "file that follow-up ticket for Kevin Tran's internet setup fee edge case I noted in ENG-214."
OE 11: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: tblStipends00001 or "Stipend Transactions") for "Kevin" or records with amount 185 using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. Discovery: Kevin Lee at GreenStack Energy submitted a $185 Internet expense (recStipend0000099, Approval Status: Pending). This is the "other Kevin with a pending expense" referenced in the prompt - same amount ($185) and same category (Internet) as Kevin Tran's internet setup fee.
OE 12: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for duplicate receipts - searching for amount 185 or "Andre Washington" or checking receipt hashes using airtable_search_records. Discovery: Andre Washington at Mosaic Robotics submitted a $185 Internet expense (recStipend0000100, Approval Status: Pending) with receipt hash a3f7c8d1e9b2f4a6c8d0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8b0c2d4e6f8a0b2c4d6e8f0a2b4 - identical to Kevin Tran's ergonomic keyboard receipt (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard). This is a suspicious duplicate: the same PDF file reused for a different expense category ($185 internet vs $89 keyboard) across two different client companies.
OE 13: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for "Maria Santos" or "standing desk" using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records. Discovery: Maria Santos's $550 standing desk expense (recStip000000018) still has Approval Status: "Rejected" with Review Notes: "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot (stale $400 default limit). Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment. Pending manual correction." The rejection has NOT been corrected yet. The prompt asks "check whether Maria Santos's standing desk rejection has been corrected yet."
OE 14: Send a Slack message to Dmitri (moveops_dmitri_volkov) using conversations_add_message (DM or fallback to #engineering C003) notifying him about the Kevin Tran edge case ticket filed and/or the duplicate receipt finding (Kevin Lee's matching $185 Internet expense and Andre Washington's matching receipt hash). The prompt says "If you file the ticket or find a duplicate, notify Dmitri on Slack about the update so he can track it."
OE 15: Search Slack messages for "CloudWatch" or "alarms" or "budget tags" using conversations_search_messages, or search emails for "CloudWatch" or "AWS alerts" using search_emails. Discovery: Lena posted in #engineering (C003): "Just pushed the new CloudWatch alarms and budget tags for the relocation pipeline. Ready for review." Dmitri replied in the same thread: "Looks good in the staging account, let's hold on prod deployment until Marcus approves the budget metrics." The alarms are live in staging only - NOT in production. Production deployment is blocked pending Marcus Thorne's approval of the budget metrics.
OE 16: Since the CloudWatch alarms are in staging only, reply to Dmitri's thread in #engineering (C003, thread_ts: 1777047136.000000) using conversations_add_message, letting him know Lena will follow up with Marcus on the budget approval this week. The prompt says "If they're still in staging, reply to that thread in #engineering letting Dmitri know I'll follow up with Marcus on the budget approval this week."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email-mosaic-schema-v24' and received a successful response with a new email_id returned.
✗Run #3The agent used send_email (not reply_to_email) to contact Mosaic. The criterion specifically requires a reply_to_email tool call referencing email-mosaic-schema-v24. The email email-mosaic-schema-v24 (from ops@mosaicrobotics.com to lena@moveops.com about v2.4 failures) was present in the search results, but the agent sent a new email to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com via send_email rather than replying to the specific email thread.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email-mosaic-schema-v24' and the tool returned a successful result with a new email_id ('78dce6a8ed094282a65038f9dcbfdd29').

2 The reply to Mosaic (visible in reply_to_email parameters) asks for specific examples of the failures they are seeing after the schema v2.4 update (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply content explicitly requests: exact payloads being rejected, error messages/rejection reasons, timestamps, and v2.3→v2.4 schema diff (field names, types, structure changes).
✓Run #2The reply content explicitly asks for: (1) specific examples of false rejections with expense IDs, amounts, and rejection reasons, (2) a sample v2.4 payload to reproduce, and (3) error messages or codes returned by the API since the update.
✓Run #3Although the wrong tool was used (send_email instead of reply_to_email), the agent's email to Mosaic explicitly asks for 'specific failure examples — screenshots, submission IDs, or employee names' and lists three specific types of information to provide. The outcome of requesting concrete failure details was achieved, even if the delivery mechanism differed.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The reply content explicitly asks for 'specific failure examples' including exact payloads, error messages/rejection codes, timestamps, and whether failures affect all policy configs or specific ones.
✓Run #6The reply content explicitly asks for: 1) specific payload examples, 2) exact error messages/rejection codes, 3) a timeline of which submissions fail, and 4) which policy types are affected. This clearly satisfies the requirement to ask for specific failure examples.
3 A Slack message was sent to Samira (moveops_samira_tariq) via conversations_add_message (DM or #engineering C003 or similar channel) and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✗✓—✓✓ 80%
✓Run #1After initial failures with @moveops_samira_tariq and moveops_samira_tariq channel IDs, the agent successfully sent a message to channel C003 tagging @samira.tariq, which returned ok:true with a message timestamp.
✗Run #2All Slack DM attempts to Samira (@samira.tariq and moveops_samira_tariq) returned {ok: false, error: channel_not_found}. Samira was notified via email instead, but the criterion requires a successful Slack message via conversations_add_message.
✓Run #3After failed DM attempts (channel_not_found), the agent successfully sent a message to C003 (#engineering) tagging @Samira. The tool_call_result_19.json shows ok: true with a message targeting C003, which is the #engineering channel per the criteria description. Tool returned success.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The agent successfully posted to channel C003 (#engineering) tagging @Samira, and the tool returned ok:true with a message timestamp.
✓Run #6Direct DM attempts to '@moveops_samira_tariq' and 'moveops_samira_tariq' failed with 'channel_not_found'. However, the agent then posted to #engineering (C003) tagging @Samira, which returned ok:true. The criterion explicitly allows '#engineering C003 or similar channel', so this passes.
